title: hieracium laevigatum subsp. keldii
intro: Hieracium laevigatum subsp. keldii, commonly known as Keld's Hawkweed, is a flowering plant species belonging to the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Eastern European folk medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation. The plant's health benefits include its ability to act as an antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent, which can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Hieracium laevigatum subsp. keldii can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
